✨Client Success Stories✨ (Check link in bio for FREE resources)

💡Do YOU want to be featured? Share your success story using #WonsultingWinners ! We'd love to shout you all out ❤️

MEET: Alejandra Huamán 📌
"Wonsulting first helped me with my resume. I can see an incredible improvement from my previous version to the one edited by Wonsulting, specially results. But most importantly asking me the right questions to fill those gaps with impacting results and actions words. The interview prep helped me understand the importance of curiosity and non-verbal communication, the tone and energy you transmit while articulating the interview questions. I also found resources that helped me structure my answers from IG and TikTok and Wonsulting's deck sample was really helpful (Facebook Proposal). And those examples from Wonsulting in the YouTube channel. There I could see how using the right words within 1-2 min can make an impact in your career. The interview process from my current job, it felt like a conversation 60%. I felt optimistic from the beginning, it was all or nothing. My Canadian visa was about to expire, and got the job last weekday possible, Friday about 7pm. I was in the job search from Jan 21, and focused 100% from May 21, 2 exciting opportunities from June (#1 multiple rounds, at least 4, #2 3 steps around 2 weeks) got the job early September from the second opportunity."

✨ If YOU need help on your job search, check out our services from the link in our bio to help land you a job in 2022!

💡Q1: You’ll be able to track all your applications, interviews, and offers in 1 place, automatically. Yes it’ll be free. 

💡Q2: We’ll tell you where you’re stuck in the job search process and provide recommendations.  
Give us a few months and we’re going to simplify your job search like you’ve never seen before. 

So excited to have you along on this journey!! Comment below if you’re as pumped as we are!

PMSI Strategy rescinded a new graduate's job offer. Tobias Temple-Smith was set to start in September, but the firm said AI would be doing the role instead. This is a wake-up call: AI is reshaping entry-level jobs, so new grads need to adapt their skills now.

Source: The Times (https://www.thetimes.com/business/companies-markets/article/graduate-jobs-alan-milburn-neets-wmgsjw7df)

A federal judge just blocked the Trump administration's $100,000 H-1B visa fee. This ruling means a massive cost barrier for H-1B visa applicants and employers, especially in tech and healthcare, has been removed. If you're an international job seeker or a company looking to hire skilled foreign talent, this is a significant development for your career path or hiring strategy.

Source: CNBC (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/08/trump-h1b-visa-fee-blocks.html)

The May 2026 Jobs Report shows 172,000 new jobs and a 4.3% unemployment rate. But don't let the headline fool you. The reality is a 'low-hire, low-fire' market, with long-term unemployment on the rise for many. This means job searches are getting harder, so target your applications to where growth is, like Private Education & Health Services.

Source: Indeed Hiringlab (https://www.hiringlab.org/2026/06/05/may-2026-jobs-report-one-strong-headline-but-two-realities/)

Getting ghosted by recruiters is hitting job seekers hard in 2026. Gen Z is especially feeling it, facing 8.3% unemployment as automated systems make things worse. Don't take it personally. Experts say follow up once, then move on and focus on warm outreach instead. Protect your mental health. 

Source: Newsweek (https://www.newsweek.com/ghosted-recruiters-survive-job-search-11972969)

Meta just announced details on over 2,000 layoffs in California, impacting its Menlo Park and Playa Vista offices. These cuts are part of 8,000 global reductions, driven by efficiency goals and a big push into AI. If you're looking at tech, it's clear AI skills are becoming essential across the board. 

Source: LA Times (https://www.latimes.com/business/story/2026-06-02/meta-provides-details-about-layoffs-in-playa-vista-menlo-park)

Silicon Valley firm Box just created 13 entirely new job types because of AI, and they're actively hiring. This goes against the layoff trends we've been seeing across the industry. If you're looking to get into AI, Box is adding positions like AI architect and AI solutions manager. It shows AI is creating new opportunities for career growth. 

Source: Livemint (https://www.livemint.com/companies/news/this-silicon-valley-company-created-13-new-job-types-because-of-ai-what-are-they-why-the-firm-is-still-hiring-11780364926667.html)

Image: New York Times

EY's new report says AI is going to permanently change India's IT services and skilled labor force. This means big shifts for global employment, potentially displacing traditional white-collar roles. If you're in tech or white-collar roles, especially in IT, now is the time to look at upskilling. AI isn't just coming. It's here and shaping entire job markets.

Source: Economic Times (https://m.economictimes.com/tech/artificial-intelligence/ai-may-impact-lucrative-job-market-linked-to-it-services-ey-report/articleshow/131408084.cms)

Australia is overhauling its employment services for over a million jobseekers. They're ending the requirement to submit 'endless' and 'pointless' job applications. This move towards personalized support highlights a global pain point for job seekers. It's a good reminder: focus on quality applications, not just quantity.

Source: The Guardian (https://www.theguardian.com/australia-news/2026/may/27/amanda-rishworth-says-jobseekers-will-no-longer-be-forced-to-submit-endless-pointless-job-applications)

Good news for new grads: Small businesses are hiring almost 1 million by 2026. Many of these are "AI-proof" roles like field managers, not just AI engineers. If you're a job seeker, building practical, hands-on skills is more important than ever.

Source: Fortune (https://fortune.com/2026/05/01/one-million-new-grads-hired-small-businesses-2026-hottest-jobs-ai-proof-service-technicians/)
